qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.title: OTP tokens logged in plaintext when debug mode enabled
qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.advisory_match_reason: This finding concerns unsanitized debug logging of OTP command arguments in the CLI dispatcher, not the advisory's unvalidated downloadPackageManager() version path used to write outside VP_HOME.

qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.title: Unbounded package download and extraction enables resource exhaustion DoS
qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.advisory_match_reason: This finding concerns missing package download and archive extraction resource limits, not the advisory's path traversal through options.version in downloadPackageManager().

target_advisory_judgement.reason: No final qualifying finding identifies the advisory-described root cause: an untrusted version string flowing into downloadPackageManager() and escaping VP_HOME/package_manager/<pm>/ through path traversal before directory replacement and shim writes.
evidence: Advisory GHSA-33r3-4whc-44c2 describes CWE-22 path traversal in vite-plus/binding downloadPackageManager() via options.version.
vuln_otp_logging reports CWE-532 plaintext OTP disclosure through debug logging in crates/vite_global_cli/src/shim/dispatch.rs.
vuln_unbounded_download_dos reports CWE-400 resource exhaustion from missing download and archive extraction limits in install/download/request paths.

Neither final qualifying report names or describes downloadPackageManager(), raw version path construction under VP_HOME, removal/replacement of an escaped target directory, or executable shim writes outside the cache root.
